records.name: Unauthorized privileged action
records.description: A privileged entry point such as mint, burn, pause, or administrative update is callable by an unauthorized actor.
records.security_property: Only authorized actors can execute privileged operations.
records.test_objective: Verify that unauthorized callers cannot execute privileged functions.
records.countermeasure: Use role-based access control, owner checks, and least-privilege permissions.

records.name: ERC-20 approval race condition
records.description: A spender exploits transaction ordering around approve() to consume both old and new allowances.
records.security_property: Allowance updates must not allow unintended double spending.
records.test_objective: Verify that allowance changes cannot be exploited through front-running.
records.countermeasure: Use increase/decrease allowance or require resetting allowance to zero before changing it.

records.name: Reentrancy during value or token transfer
records.description: A callback or external value transfer allows re-entry before state is finalized.
records.security_property: State must be updated before external calls that may transfer control.
records.test_objective: Verify that a malicious receiver cannot re-enter and consume state twice.
records.countermeasure: Apply checks-effects-interactions and/or a reentrancy guard.

records.name: Token accounting invariant violation
records.description: A token operation incorrectly updates balances, ownership, supply, assets, or shares.
records.security_property: Token operations must preserve standard-specific accounting invariants.
records.test_objective: Verify that state transitions preserve token accounting invariants.
records.countermeasure: Check preconditions and assert post-state accounting invariants.
